extraction.one_line_summary: PEDERSEN Mads won from a reduced sprint after KRAGH ANDERSEN Søren's late attack softened the field and Trek-Segafredo executed a dominant leadout via STUYVEN Jasper
tactical_patterns: KRAGH ANDERSEN Søren attacked twice late-race gaining 30 seconds, tenderizing field before reduced sprint favored climber-sprinters
Trek-Segafredo placed STUYVEN leadout perfectly on uphill finish, full leadout execution beats raw speed when pure sprinters drop
Bonus sprint at 20km with ROGLIČ attacking shows GC teams willing to burn matches before technical finale
HOLMES Matthew's KOM jersey defended by teammate DEGANDT Thomas taking intermediate points, passive jersey defense via teamwork

rider_signals.PEDERSEN Mads: positive: first WorldTour win in extended period suggests form building, handles uphill sprint finish better than pure sprinters
rider_signals.KRAGH ANDERSEN Søren: positive: double late-race attacks gaining significant time indicates exceptional condition beyond typical classics form
rider_signals.STUYVEN Jasper: positive: executed leadout on uphill finish reveals tactical flexibility beyond classics rouleur role
rider_signals.LAPORTE Christophe: neutral: crashed inside 1km but retained yellow via same-time rule, suggests positioning vulnerability in reduced sprints
rider_signals.JAKOBSEN Fabio: negative: dropped before finale on lumpy circuit, confirms pure sprinters vulnerable even moderate late gradients
rider_signals.BENNETT Sam: negative: dropped with fastest sprinters on lumpy approach, confirms climbing limitations persist
rider_signals.ROGLIČ Primož: neutral: contested intermediate bonus sprint for 2 seconds shows GC aggression but no decisive advantage gained
